title: Fix: targeted.py project_root fallback silently uses project_id as path
analysis:
In targeted.py, both reconcile_task() (line 86-87) and reconcile_bulk_tasks() (line 353-354) silently fall back to using project_id (a logical name like 'dark_factory') as project_root (should be an absolute filesystem path) when project_root is empty. This violates the convention that project_root must be an absolute path. All production callers in task_interceptor.py always pass project_root explicitly after MCP-layer validation via _validate_project_root(), so this is dead code — but the fallback is incorrect and could cause task tree corruption if ever triggered.

Fix: Replace the silent fallback with a ValueError, matching the validation pattern in _base_args() (raises ValueError) and _validate_project_root() (checks non-empty + os.path.isabs). This requires adding 'import os' to targeted.py.

15 existing tests call reconcile_task/reconcile_bulk_tasks without project_root and rely on the default empty string. These must be updated to pass a valid absolute path before the validation is added, or they will break.

prerequisites.description: Update 15 existing tests in test_targeted.py that call reconcile_task() or reconcile_bulk_tasks() without project_root to pass project_root='/tmp/test'. This ensures they survive the new validation without changing test semantics — they test other behaviors (fast-path writes, causation IDs, journal logging, hints, deferral, etc.) and just need a valid project_root to avoid ValueError. Affected tests: test_on_task_done_fast_path_write, test_on_task_done_passes_causation_id, test_on_task_done_logs_run_actions, test_on_task_done_sparse_knowledge, test_on_task_done_rich_knowledge, test_on_task_done_checks_dependents, test_on_task_blocked_attaches_hints, test_on_task_cancelled_checks_subtasks, test_on_task_deferred_same_as_blocked, test_reconcile_task_failure_handling, test_done_defers_write_during_active_cycle, test_done_writes_normally_when_no_cycle, test_reads_proceed_during_active_cycle, test_blocked_proceeds_during_active_cycle, test_no_buffer_writes_normally.

steps.description: Write failing tests for reconcile_task() validation in a new section '# ── Tests for project_root validation (task-156) ──' at the end of test_targeted.py. Three tests: (1) test_reconcile_task_rejects_empty_project_root — calls with project_root='' (default), expects ValueError with match 'absolute path'. (2) test_reconcile_task_rejects_relative_project_root — calls with project_root='dark_factory' (logical name), expects ValueError. (3) test_reconcile_task_rejects_dot_project_root — calls with project_root='.', expects ValueError. All use pytest.raises(ValueError, match='absolute path').

In targeted.py reconcile_task(): add 'import os' at top of file (after 'import json'). Replace lines 85-87 (comment + if/fallback) with validation: 'if not project_root or not os.path.isabs(project_root): raise ValueError(f"project_root must be a non-empty absolute path, got: {project_root!r}")'. All step-1 tests and all pre-1 updated tests should now pass.

Write failing tests for reconcile_bulk_tasks() validation in the same section. Two tests: (1) test_reconcile_bulk_rejects_empty_project_root — calls reconcile_bulk_tasks with project_root='' (default), expects ValueError. (2) test_reconcile_bulk_rejects_relative_project_root — calls with project_root='dark_factory', expects ValueError. Both use pytest.raises(ValueError, match='absolute path').

In targeted.py reconcile_bulk_tasks(): replace lines 353-354 (comment + if/fallback) with the same ValueError validation: 'if not project_root or not os.path.isabs(project_root): raise ValueError(f"project_root must be a non-empty absolute path, got: {project_root!r}")'. All step-3 tests should now pass. Run full test suite to confirm no regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Raise ValueError instead of returning error dict
design_decisions.rationale: reconcile_task() and reconcile_bulk_tasks() are internal methods called by task_interceptor.py, not MCP tool handlers. The MCP tools layer returns error dicts (via _validate_project_root), but internal code raises ValueError (as in _base_args). ValueError is the correct pattern at this layer — it will be caught by the except block in reconcile_task (line 128) and logged, preventing silent corruption.

design_decisions.decision: Validate with os.path.isabs() in addition to non-empty check
design_decisions.rationale: Matching the _validate_project_root() pattern which checks both conditions. This catches the specific bug (logical name like 'dark_factory' is non-empty but not absolute) as well as other invalid inputs like relative paths or '.'.

design_decisions.decision: Update existing tests to pass project_root='/tmp/test' as a prerequisite
design_decisions.rationale: 15 existing tests use the default project_root='' because they test other behaviors (causation IDs, journal logging, hints, deferral). Updating them first keeps the prerequisite separate from the TDD cycle and avoids a mass breakage at step-2. The path '/tmp/test' is consistent with the config fixture's explore_codebase_root.

design_decisions.decision: Apply same fix to both reconcile_task and reconcile_bulk_tasks
design_decisions.rationale: Both methods have the identical silent fallback bug (lines 86-87 and 353-354). The task description focuses on reconcile_task but reconcile_bulk_tasks has the same pattern and should be fixed for consistency.

reuse.how: Reuse the same condition (not project_root or not os.path.isabs(project_root)) and similar error message format. Raise ValueError instead of returning error dict since this is internal code, matching _base_args() in taskmaster_client.py.

reuse.what: Existing test fixtures (reconciler, journal, mock_memory_service, etc.)
reuse.how: New validation tests reuse the same reconciler fixture. Tests use pytest.raises(ValueError, match=...) pattern.
Follow the same ValueError pattern with descriptive message including the invalid value repr.
